activationGate: Use after Archer Pilot Acceptance and after the relevant archetype/source-identity prerequisites pass. This record does not authorize current production.
coreGoal: Replace serial pilot-style production with archetype-driven batch production while preserving per-package quality, exact-file approval, rollback and auditability.

A_oneTimePilotWork: prove source/identity approval and immutable exact-file boundaries
prove sidecar/source-map schema, anchor, rootMotion, runtimeFlipX and marker vocabulary
prove board/mobile review artifacts and exact approval sequencing
capture visual-failure lessons such as near-static motion, identity drift and baked artifacts
B_reusableWork: runtime import seams and rollback structure
C_characterSpecificWork: attack or skill semantic pose progression
human visual approval of exact candidate
D_batchableWork: simple idle production across compatible units
basic attacks only within compatible biomechanics subgroups
batch exact-file approval overlay containing isolated per-package hashes
grouped runtime migration for already approved isolated packages
E_mustRemainIndividuallyReviewed: each exact package visual acceptance even when displayed in one batch review
any package with identity drift, near-static motion or repeated visual rejection

batchSizeGuidelines.notes: Broadest safe batching; keep identity and weight differences in per-package config.
Group by locomotion biomechanics; markers and anchor/support remain package-specific.
Only when dedicated hit motion is prioritized; runtime hit-flash already exists.
Workflow can batch; final poses must remain class/character distinct.
Only when cast skeleton and marker semantics are truly compatible.

continueBatchWhen: all units share a proven archetype pipeline
technical package schema and validator support all candidates
motion semantics are compatible enough to share production tooling without cloning poses
no candidate requires a unique runtime/gameplay change
review artifacts can show every package clearly at board/mobile scale
each package can be approved, rejected, reverted and migrated independently
splitToIndividualWhen: one unit shows identity drift or near-static motion
marker timing is ambiguous or unique
a boss or signature skill needs bespoke timing
a package fails visual review twice
a source/master changes after batch production starts
a runtime change would be needed for only one candidate

commonTechnicalValidator.design: One reusable engine driven by package config; avoid copying a full validator file per character when checks are structurally identical.

characterSpecificConfig: expected marker names and semantic frames
validatorArchitecturePlan.rule: Common technical PASS never overrides motion-specific or human visual failure.

automaticForEveryPackage: marker diagnostic only when the package has a semantic marker
complexOnly: comparison diagnostic against reference or rejected predecessor
special transition diagnostic when risk is known
batchArtifacts: one combined contact-sheet index/page linking or grouping all package contact sheets
one combined preview page/grid optimized for phone review
per-package exact artifact/hash manifest remains separate
reviewArtifactPipeline.rule: Do not manually create redundant review artifacts when the standard set already proves the gate.

batchApprovalStrategy.humanReview: A user may review and approve several simple packages in one combined batch view.
batchApprovalStrategy.exactApproval: Exact hashes and semantics remain isolated per package even when one approval record/PR contains multiple approved packages.
batchApprovalStrategy.partialFailure: If one package fails, reject/redo only that package; passed packages may proceed if ancestry and approval records are independent.

fastPathRecommended: one Batch Production PR for 3-6 compatible units/packages
one Batch Exact-File Approval overlay PR containing only packages explicitly approved by the user
one Grouped Runtime Migration PR for approved packages, with per-unit/state rollback isolation
avoidByDefault: one readiness PR per simple motion
one production PR per simple package when several compatible packages can be isolated in one PR
copying Archer pilot bureaucracy to every unit
separatePRRequiredWhen: a failed package needs an isolated redo/version

rollbackAuditRules: every unit/state package has its own version, manifest and hashes
batch PR paths must remain package-isolated
approval is recorded per package even if decisions are made in one review session
runtime mapping must be reversible per unit/state without changing approved bytes
a failed package must not force byte changes in sibling packages
